rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., VS. CASTRO POINT LOUNGE, LLC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition was filed. The motion is granted. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $390. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN CHAN VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF SUSAN CHAN's Motion For Attorney's Fees. The motion for attorney fees is denied without prejudice as premature. The determination of who is the prevailing party must await the conclusion of this action, in which there can be only one prevailing party. See Civ.Code, § 1717, subds. (a), (b)(1).) See Roberts v Packard, Packard & Johnson, -- Cal. Rptr.--, 2013 WL 3357120 (Cal. App. July 3, 2013); Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc., 206 Cal. App. 4th 515, 535 (2012). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ICHO VS. BALDWIN BOK YUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs BALDWIN YUNG and WORLDWIDE CAPITAL ASIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer with 15 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead more facts to support the alter ego allegations. The remaining grounds for demurrer are overruled. None of the alleged contracts requires consummation of a hotel transaction as a condition precedent for payment. In ruling on a demurrer, the Court accepts plaintiff's interpretation of the contract pursuant to Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. The Court also rejects the agreement to agree argument. The MOU does not facially disclose an agreement to agree. The Court rejects defendant's argument regarding a broker's license. Plaintiff is no obligated to plead around the affirmative defense of illegality and a factual issue remains regarding whether the plaintiff had the required license or engaged in negotiations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LIVE NATION MERCHANDISE, INC., A DELAWARE VS. STANLEY G MILLER, AKA STANLEY MOUSE, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTs STANLEY G MILLER, AKA STANLEY MOUSE, AN INDIVIDUAL, THE ALTON KELLEY AND MARGUERITE TROUSDALE KELLEY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege standing and a cause of action for breach of contract. A general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. The First Amended Complaint is not barred on its face by the statute of limitations. Defendants raise issues of fact that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. Defendants' alternative motion to strike is denied. As pled, the subject allegations are not irrelevant, false or improper. Defendants raise issues of fact that are not the proper subject of motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARIEL ROSE VS. LOCAL: MISSION EATERY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 8, Part II of tentative ruling, Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions As to the individual defendants Milgrim and Des Voignes: Granted as to Request for Production nos. 7, Form Interrogatory nos. 4.1, 200.1, 214.1. Denied as to Special Interrogatory nos. 5, 7, 11, Form Interrogatory nos. 2.6, 12.1, 12.2, 215.1 As to the individual defendant Griffin: Granted as to Special Interrogatory no. 3, Form Interrogatory no. 4.1, 214.1. Denied as to Form Interrogatory no. 12.1, 12.2, 214.1, 215.1. Special Interrogatory no. 3 is granted subject to the parties meeting and conferring to better define the relevant documents. Sanctions: Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is denied. There is no written record of when settlement discussions reached an impasse, and the declarations of counsel are contradictory as to that date. If, as alleged, settlement discussions lasted through July 3, then this motion was premature. Defendants seem to have reasonably complied with discovery requests and substantively responded to meet and confer letters in a not untimely manner. A large majority of the items granted above have only to do with the production of the insurance policy, but defendants have previously identified the coverage limits of that policy and answered other policy questions except the policy number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ARIEL ROSE VS. LOCAL: MISSION EATERY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compe Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Deft'S And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S And There Atorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 8, Part I of tentative ruling, PLAINTIFF ARIEL ROSE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Deft'S And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S And There Atorney Of Record Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part as follows: As to Local Mission Eatery: Grant as to Request for Production no. 7, Form Interrogatory nos. 4.1, 214.1 Denied as to Special Interrogatory nos. 5, 10, 12, Form Interrogatory nos. 12.1, 12.2, 215.1. For Request for Production no. 7, the insurance policy must be produced. For Special Interrogatory no. 5, this is essential a request to identify ESI. Defendant is only obligated to state the form in which intends to produce the information. There is no statutory obligation to Bates stamp. For Special Interrogatory no. 10, the documents are described in sufficient detail to be used as a basis for a deposition subpoena or request for production. For Form Interrogatory no. 12.1, the term "incident" is too vague in the context of this interrogatory which is more applicable to an auto accident than it is to a series of events. For Form Interrogatory no. 12.2, Plaintiff is not prejudiced where nine possible witnesses are listed. Here, revealing which of the witnesses was interviewed compromises attorney work product.

rulings.Case Title: PHI TRUONG VS. SU LING KONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT SU KONG's DEMURRER TO1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for negligent entrustment of a motor vehicle. "Liability for negligent entrustment is determined by applying general principles of negligence, and ordinarily it is for the jury to determine whether the owner has exercised the required degree of care." (Allen v. Toledo (1980) 109 Cal. App. 3d 415, 421.) Defendant raises questions of fact that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
